votes.reason: The Python code correctly translates all helper functions and the main specification, preserving the exact logic for counting vowels and checking the last character.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq logic, accurately counting vowels and checking if the last character is 'y' or 'Y'.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, accurately translating the string operations, vowel counting, and the check for whether the last character is 'y'.

The Python code correctly implements all the Coq definitions: ascii_eqb compares characters, vowelb checks for vowels (aeiouAEIOU), yb checks for y/Y, count_vowels_excluding_y sums vowels in the string, last_is_y checks if the last character is y/Y, and vowels_count_spec checks if n equals the vowel count plus 1 if the string ends in y. All concrete behaviors match.

Matches Coq: counts only aeiou/AEIOU per character and adds 1 if the last character is y/Y; empty and other cases align.
Python mirrors the Coq logic: counts only a/e/i/o/u (case-insensitive), excludes y from the count, and adds 1 if the last character is y or Y; matches Coq on all ASCII inputs.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: it counts only a/e/i/o/u (both cases), treats y/Y as a vowel only if it is the last character, handles empty strings the same way, and compares characters by equality. Thus results match on all concrete strings.
